mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy that lowers discount rates, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in CPIAUCSL above 250 indicates inflationary pressures that prompt central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 250, or the Rank IC drops below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and industrial production signals robust economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings and investor confidence.
failure_condition: The combined effect of GDPC1 and INDPRO growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment rates (UNRATE > 4%) indicate weak labor market conditions that depress consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4%, or the Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 5d returns in > 60% of cases, or the Rank IC drops below 0.03 over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through sectoral growth and wealth effects.
failure_condition: The combined effect of HOUST and RSAFS growth fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle, indicated by rising FEDFUNDS, leads to higher discount rates and reduced equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS rises by 0.5% monthly, or the Rank IC drops below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE < 3%) signals a tight labor market that drives wage inflation and corporate cost pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE falls below 3%, or the Rank IC drops below 0.03 over any 1-year period.
